title: Highest temperature in Seoul on July 18?
description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 18 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Incheon Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders see the tight clustering around 27–30°C for Seoul’s July 18 high temperature as reflecting typical midsummer monsoon conditions and model consensus from the Korea Meteorological Administration. Persistent humidity and frequent cloud cover or showers limit daytime heating, keeping most forecasts near the long-term July average of roughly 30°C while preventing extremes above 31°C. Recent model runs show only modest day-to-day variability tied to the timing of rain bands, which explains why no single outcome exceeds 24% implied probability. Updated short-range guidance expected in the next 24–48 hours will likely narrow the range as land-sea breeze and precipitation timing become clearer.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Recent official forecasts position 14°C as the most probable daily maximum for Wellington on July 18, aligning with the market's leading 50.5% implied probability.** July marks mid-winter in the Southern Hemisphere, when Wellington's temperate maritime climate typically delivers average daily highs of 12–14°C, driven by prevailing westerly winds, frequent cloud cover, and limited solar heating. NIWA's May–July 2026 seasonal outlook indicates temperatures are about equally likely to finish near or below average across the region, with occasional cold snaps possible under high pressure. Current short-range guidance from sources such as the Met Office and local models points to a stable 14°C high on the 18th, with light northerly flow potentially allowing a modest 15°C reading if mixing is stronger than expected. Model consensus shows low variability, supporting the combined 84% probability on 14–15°C outcomes while keeping higher or lower extremes below 10% combined. Traders appear to be weighting the latest deterministic runs and climatological baselines, which favor these narrow thresholds ahead of any final pre-event model updates.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 18 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Tokyo Haneda Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/jp/tokyo/RJTT.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tokyo's mid-July climatology centers on highs of 30–32°C under the influence of the subtropical Pacific high, with high humidity amplifying perceived heat via the urban heat island effect. Short-range ensemble guidance from the Japan Meteorological Agency and global models shows modest spread around 29–31°C for July 18, driven by variable cloud cover, possible scattered showers, and steering flow that could either enhance or suppress daytime warming. The tightly clustered market odds reflect this forecast uncertainty, with differentiation hinging on exact timing of any frontal passage or sea-breeze development; stronger onshore flow or increased convection would favor the lower outcomes near 29°C, while clearer, more stable conditions would push toward 31°C or above. Updated model runs and JMA guidance through the 18th will be the key near-term catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Persistent summer heat across eastern China, combined with an incoming pattern of increased cloud cover and thunderstorms, is shaping the close spread in trader probabilities for Shanghai’s July 18 high temperature.** Recent observations show daily maxima of 37–39 °C in the city, consistent with a broader regional heat episode that prompted health alerts. Forecast guidance indicates a modest cooling trend by the 18th, with models converging on a daytime peak near 35 °C under overcast skies and scattered storms that increase humidity and reduce solar heating.  

The tight clustering around 35–37 °C (market-implied odds of 16.5–26.5 %) reflects uncertainty in storm timing and intensity: earlier or stronger convective activity would cap the high closer to 35 °C, while delayed or lighter precipitation could allow readings to reach 36–37 °C. Lower-probability outcomes above 38 °C or below 34 °C are discounted because current model consensus and the expected weather regime make extremes less likely within the two-day forecast window. Updated model runs and official briefings from Chinese meteorological services over the next 24–48 hours will be the key catalysts for any shifts in these probabilities.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest forecasts from global and regional models, aligned with Meteorological Service Singapore guidance, position the July 18 daily maximum near 32°C as the modal outcome, with trader probabilities clustered around 31–33°C reflecting uncertainty in afternoon convection timing. Southwest monsoon flow favors scattered thundery showers that can limit peak solar heating, though breaks in cloud cover or reduced rainfall could push readings to 33°C or briefly higher. Above-normal July temperatures tied to emerging El Niño influences provide a warm baseline, consistent with recent station highs near 32.7°C, while typical July climatology centers on 31–32°C maxima. Updated model runs and official nowcasts over the next 48 hours will refine the precise threshold before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent forecasts for Taipei indicate typical mid-July subtropical conditions with afternoon highs potentially reaching 35–37°C, driven by strong solar heating under variable cloud cover and high humidity from the East Asian monsoon. Trader sentiment clusters tightly around 36–38°C because historical July extremes in Taipei often exceed 35°C during clear spells, while recent Central Weather Administration guidance notes persistent warmth without strong typhoon or heavy rain signals that would cap readings. Updated model outputs and observations over the next 48 hours will likely refine these narrow spreads.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent short-range forecasts from models and agencies place Chengdu’s July 18 maximum near 33–35 °C under typical mid-summer subtropical conditions, with scattered thunderstorms and high humidity expected to limit peak heating. The Sichuan Basin’s topography often traps moist air and moderates daytime maxima when cloud cover or precipitation arrives, while clearer periods allow brief spikes toward 36 °C. Current ensemble guidance shows modest spread in timing and coverage of convective activity, which directly influences whether the official station reading settles at 33 °C, 34 °C, or 35 °C. Historical July data indicate these values fall within the normal interquartile range, with occasional excursions above 37 °C only under stronger subsidence. Traders are therefore weighting the most recent model updates and any revisions ahead of the 48-hour window.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest ensemble forecasts from the China Meteorological Administration show a tight cluster around 30–32°C for Shenzhen’s July 18 maximum, driven by persistent subtropical ridge influence and moist southerly flow from the South China Sea. Model spread arises from uncertainty in daytime convective timing and cloud cover, which can reduce solar heating and cap peaks near 29–30°C or allow brief clearing that pushes readings to 32–33°C. Typical July climatology features 31–33°C highs amid high humidity, but recent regional rain patterns have introduced variability in boundary-layer moisture and stability. Traders therefore assign the highest implied probability to 31°C while acknowledging that the next 48-hour model runs could shift the mode if precipitation arrives earlier or later than currently projected.

eventMetadata.context_description: Forecast models from the Korea Meteorological Administration indicate high uncertainty for Busan’s July 18 maximum temperature, with ensemble spreads clustering around 29–31°C amid typical mid-July East Asian monsoon conditions. Elevated atmospheric moisture and potential cloud cover or thundershowers can suppress daytime heating through reduced solar insolation, while coastal sea breezes may moderate peaks near the historical July average of 29–30°C. Recent short-range guidance highlights variability in humidity and localized precipitation timing as key differentiators among adjacent thresholds, keeping market-implied probabilities closely matched as traders weigh model consensus against coastal microclimate effects.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the Qingdao July 18 maximum-temperature market centers on 28–31 °C because typical mid-July synoptic patterns over the Yellow Sea produce moderate onshore flow that caps daytime heating near the long-term average high of 27–28 °C. eventMetadata.context_description: The closely bunched market odds around 31–33 °C reflect the typical July climatology for Manila under the southwest monsoon, or habagat, which currently drives scattered afternoon convection and variable cloud cover across Metro Manila. eventMetadata.context_description: Current ensemble forecasts from the Met Office and ECMWF place London's July 18 maximum near 23–26 °C, with 24 °C holding the highest implied probability as models converge on modest Atlantic-driven cooling after earlier July warmth. Key differentiating factors include the timing of a weak frontal passage, variable cloud cover that limits solar heating, and boundary-layer moisture affecting the afternoon peak. Small shifts in low-level wind direction or clearance of stratiform cloud can swing the daily high by 1–2 °C, explaining the tight clustering around 24–26 °C while lower and higher outcomes remain marginal. Updated model runs and Met Office guidance on July 17 will further refine these probabilities ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent model consensus from Météo-France and European ensembles shows a moderating high-pressure ridge over northern France, with southwesterly flow advecting milder maritime air toward Paris by July 18. This setup favors afternoon maxima near 27–29 °C at standard observing sites, tempered by possible increased cloud cover and a slight uptick in boundary-layer mixing. Urban heat-island effects could add 1–2 °C locally, yet forecast spread arises mainly from subtle differences in timing of any weak frontal passage and insolation duration. Traders therefore cluster probabilities around the 28 °C and 29 °C bins, reflecting the narrow range between official guidance and ensemble variability two days ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description:
**Current short-range model guidance from ECMWF and GFS ensembles positions Ankara’s July 18 daily maximum near 30–31 °C under persistent high pressure, clear skies, and light northeasterly flow across the Anatolian plateau.** These conditions favor strong daytime solar heating with minimal cloud cover or precipitation, aligning with Ankara’s July climatological average high of approximately 31 °C. Recent forecasts from BBC Weather and timeanddate.com explicitly project a 31 °C high for the 18th, while broader monthly outlooks show a 28.5–33.3 °C envelope centered on 32 °C. 

Market-implied odds cluster tightly around 29–31 °C because ensemble spreads remain narrow this close to the event, reflecting stable synoptic forcing rather than significant model disagreement or incoming frontal systems. Minor downward revisions in some runs (favoring 29–30 °C) versus slight upward potential under drier boundary-layer mixing explain the near-even split among the three leading outcomes, with traders discounting extremes below 28 °C or above 32 °C given the absence of heatwave or cooling signals in the latest guidance. Official observations will resolve the contract based on the 12:00 UTC maximum at the primary Ankara station.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent official forecasts from sources like the India Meteorological Department and global models point to a highest temperature in Lucknow on July 18 most likely falling in the low-to-mid 30s Celsius, with monsoon cloud cover and scattered rainfall as the dominant cooling influence. Above-normal seasonal temperatures are expected across northern India, yet active monsoon conditions—typical for mid-July in Uttar Pradesh—introduce uncertainty through variable precipitation and reduced solar heating, keeping daily maxima near climatological averages of 32–34°C. Model spreads reflect this, with some runs projecting brief monsoon breaks allowing 34–35°C while others favor heavier showers capping readings at 32°C or below. Traders appear to weigh these near-term forecast updates and historical July variability heavily, as the tightly bunched probabilities around 32–34°C highlight genuine uncertainty ahead of final observational data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent model consensus from European and global forecasting systems indicates Madrid will likely see a peak temperature of 37°C on July 18 under a stable high-pressure ridge with light winds and clear skies, supporting the market's strong 59.5% implied probability for that outcome. Short-range guidance shows daytime maxima climbing from 35–36°C on July 16–17, with limited moisture and no significant frontal activity to moderate readings. The narrower probabilities for 36°C (24.5%) and 38°C (16.0%) reflect ensemble spread and typical model uncertainty in boundary-layer mixing two days ahead. Spain's record-warm first half of 2026 provides climatological context but does not alter the immediate synoptic setup. Updated NWP runs and any last-minute adjustments to the thermal ridge will be the key catalysts for shifts in trader positioning before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current ensemble forecasts from the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) and local IMGW models indicate a moderating Atlantic air mass will limit Warsaw’s July 18 maximum to the 24–26 °C range, with thundery showers possible during peak heating hours that reduce insolation and cap afternoon temperatures. This follows a late-June heatwave that produced Poland’s all-time record of 40.5 °C but has since yielded to cooler-than-average July conditions across central Europe. Traders have concentrated probability on 25–26 °C outcomes because model runs show modest uncertainty around cloud cover and convective timing, while higher thresholds remain low-probability given the post-heatwave pattern and typical July climatology of 24–25 °C. Updated model guidance and IMGW morning observations on July 17 will provide the next key data points before market resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ensemble forecasts from the Finnish Meteorological Institute and ECMWF place Helsinki’s July 18 daytime maximum near 23–25 °C under partly cloudy skies, light northeasterly flow, and modest solar insolation. A building Scandinavian ridge supports gradual warming above the July climatological mean of ~21 °C, yet Baltic Sea breezes and residual boundary-layer moisture limit further rises. Short-range model consensus remains tight two days ahead, with resolution hinging on the precise peak at official stations; any increase in cloud cover or shift in wind direction could favor the 24 °C or lower brackets that together hold nearly 40 % of market-implied probability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Latest National Weather Service forecasts for Atlanta on July 18 point to a maximum near 91°F under mostly sunny skies with light northwest flow and a modest afternoon thunderstorm chance after peak heating. This consensus, drawn from official model runs and climatological norms of 89–90°F for mid-July, underpins the near-even market split between the 90–91°F and 92–93°F bins. Key differentiating factors include the precise timing of any convective clouds, which can shave 1–2°F off the high, versus clearer conditions allowing brief exceedance of 92°F; dew-point trends and boundary-layer mixing also add small uncertainty. Updated model guidance and NWS briefings expected overnight will likely refine these probabilities ahead of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: National Weather Service forecasts for Miami International Airport point to a maximum near 91–93°F on July 18 under typical mid-summer high-pressure conditions with light easterly flow and afternoon sea-breeze development that often caps peak readings. This aligns with the 30-year July normal of 91°F and recent observations showing daily highs of 91–94°F amid a broader warm pattern across Florida without an intense heat dome. Model consensus favors limited cloud cover and modest moisture, keeping the most likely outcomes in the 90–93°F brackets that together exceed 80% implied probability, while drier air or delayed convection could briefly push readings into the mid-90s. The next NWS point forecast update and any revised model runs ahead of the daily maximum will serve as the primary near-term catalysts for shifts in trader positioning.

eventMetadata.context_description: **Persistent high pressure and clear skies are positioning Denver for a high near 94–96°F on July 18, aligning with the market’s leading 94–95°F (46%) and 96–97°F (18.2%) buckets.** Official NWS guidance and multiple private models show sunny conditions with light northeast winds and minimal afternoon moisture, allowing strong daytime radiative heating above the seasonal normal of ~90–91°F. Recent model runs have held steady in the mid-90s with low spread, reflecting a stable summer pattern of upper-level ridging across the Front Range. Any modest cooling from isolated high-based showers or slight wind shifts remains the main uncertainty, though probabilities favor staying below 98°F given the current consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Current National Weather Service and NOAA ensemble guidance for Houston on July 18 favors a daily maximum in the low-to-mid 90s under partly cloudy skies, light southerly flow, and moderate humidity, positioning the 92–93 °F bin as the market leader at 38.5 %. Typical July climatology at Intercontinental Airport shows a 1991–2020 normal high of 95 °F, with recent model runs reflecting suppressed heat advection from upstream ridging rather than a strong heat dome. Ensemble spread remains modest this close to the event, limiting upside risk above 96 °F while capping downside probability below 90 °F. Final forecast adjustments from the afternoon model cycle and any late-day convection could still shift the exact bin before resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The narrow gap between the 64-65°F (34.5%) and 66-67°F (33.0%) brackets reflects ensemble model runs from the National Weather Service placing the official KSFO high near 65°F on July 18, with modest uncertainty in marine-layer depth and afternoon clearing timing. Persistent onshore flow from the cool California Current sustains a shallow marine inversion that caps daytime heating, while any temporary weakening of the layer or brief offshore wind component could allow modest warming into the upper 60s. Historical July climatology at the airport shows average highs near 66°F, and recent NWS guidance through mid-July has favored similar mild conditions absent a strong thermal trough or wind shift.

This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Marcos A. Gelabert Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 18 Jul '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day for the Marcos A. Gelabert Intl Airport Station, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pa/panama-city/MPMG.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve until the first data point for the following date has been published on the resolution source.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Revisions to temperatures recorded within this market's timeframe will be considered until the first datapoint for the following date has been published, after which any alterations will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: The closely matched market-implied odds for 33°C and 34°C as Panama City's July 18 high reflect uncertainty in short-range forecast guidance amid the region's typical wet-season convection. Afternoon thunderstorms driven by the Intertropical Convergence Zone often limit peak temperatures through cloud cover and evaporative cooling, with historical July averages near 31–32°C at Tocumen International Airport. Small variations in model consensus on precipitation timing, boundary-layer moisture, or light wind patterns can shift the daily maximum by 1°C, while ENSO-neutral conditions provide no strong large-scale signal. Traders are monitoring the next GFS and ECMWF runs plus local Panamanian meteorological updates for clearer resolution criteria on the exact peak reading.
